rationale: The citing paper uses Zhu and Ratner (2015) as a key source for the scarcity-versus-abundance resource mindset concept in its theory development, not just as a passing background citation. It repeatedly relies on this work to justify claims about default abundance mindsets and to operationalize resource mindset via plate-size-induced fullness/emptiness in Study 1. These uses inform both the conceptual framing and an implemented manipulation in the empirical design.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Zhu and Ratner (2015) as a conceptual and methodological base for resource mindset, then builds its food-waste framework on that base. It imports the scarcity/abundance mindset logic and a related manipulation approach to study a different outcome context.
